steps.title: Spot the defining claim
steps.content: The sentence contains two independent clauses: Chambers did not play drums on “Rapper’s Delight,” and he joined the label after the song’s release. The word however is set off by the comma after Delight, and the semicolon separates the two independent clauses.

steps.title: Apply the signal to the choices
steps.content: Delight,” however; correctly places however after the first clause and uses the semicolon to join the two independent clauses. That matches the correct answer — C is correct.
